analysis.summary: Gitea is a self-hosted Git service written in Go that provides web-based Git repository hosting, issue tracking, pull requests, and continuous integration. It implements a monolithic web application architecture with SQLite, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MSSQL database support, featuring both web UI and REST API interfaces. The system uses a traditional MVC pattern with chi router for HTTP routing, XORM for database ORM, and includes built-in CI/CD capabilities through Gitea Actions.
capabilities: Hosts Git repositories with web-based browsing and file editing (web_src/js/features/repo-editor.ts, models/repo/)
Manages user authentication with multiple backends including LDAP, OAuth2, SMTP, and WebAuthn (models/auth/, modules/auth/)
Provides issue tracking with labels, milestones, assignees, and project boards (models/issues/, web_src/js/features/repo-issue.ts)
Implements pull request workflow with reviews, approvals, and merge strategies (models/pull/, web_src/js/features/repo-issue.ts)
Executes CI/CD pipelines through Gitea Actions with runner support (models/actions/, cmd/actions.go)
Serves Git operations over HTTP and SSH protocols (cmd/serv.go, modules/ssh/)
Manages organizations with team-based access control (models/organization/, web_src/js/features/org-team.ts)
Provides webhook integration for external service notifications (models/webhook/, web_src/js/features/comp/WebHookEditor.ts)
Implements Git LFS (Large File Storage) support for binary file handling (modules/lfs/, modules/lfstransfer/)
Offers package registry functionality for multiple package types (models/packages/, modules/packages/)
Provides code search with indexing capabilities using Bleve or Elasticsearch (modules/indexer/)
Manages SSH and GPG key authentication for secure Git operations (models/asymkey/)
Implements repository mirroring for external Git repositories (models/repo/, cmd/migrate.go)
Provides admin interface for user, organization, and system management (cmd/admin.go, web_src/js/features/admin/)
Supports multiple database backends with migration system (models/db/, models/migrations/)
Implements markup rendering for Markdown, AsciiDoc, and other formats (modules/markup/)
Provides real-time notifications and activity feeds (models/activities/, web_src/js/features/notification.ts)
Offers code editor with syntax highlighting using CodeMirror (web_src/js/modules/codeeditor/)
Manages file uploads and attachments with configurable storage backends (modules/storage/)
Implements rate limiting and security headers for web requests (modules/web/middleware/)
Provides repository statistics and contribution graphs (web_src/js/features/heatmap.ts, web_src/js/features/contributors.ts)
Supports repository templates and initialization from external sources (modules/repository/)
Implements backup and restore functionality for repositories and data (cmd/dump.go, cmd/restore_repo.go)
Provides multi-language support with internationalization (options/locale/, web_src/js/modules/i18n.ts)
Manages session storage with configurable backends including Redis (modules/session/, gitea.com/go-chi/session)
use_cases: Software development teams use this to host private Git repositories with web-based collaboration features
Open source projects use this as an alternative to GitHub for self-hosted Git repository management
DevOps teams use this for CI/CD automation through integrated Gitea Actions workflows
Organizations use this to maintain control over source code hosting while providing GitHub-like features
Development teams use this for code review workflows with pull requests and inline commenting
analysis.target_audience: Software developers, DevOps engineers, System administrators, Open source maintainers
technical_stack: Go 1.26.2 (go.mod)
Chi v5 HTTP router (github.com/go-chi/chi/v5 in go.mod)
XORM database ORM (xorm.io/xorm v1.3.9 in go.mod)
SQLite, PostgreSQL, MySQL, MSSQL database support (go.mod dependencies)
TypeScript 5.7.3 for frontend (package.json)
Fomantic-UI for CSS framework (web_src/fomantic/)
CodeMirror 6.x for code editing (package.json)
Docker containerization (Dockerfile, docker-compose support)
Prometheus metrics support (github.com/prometheus/client_golang in go.mod)
Redis for caching and sessions (github.com/redis/go-redis/v9 in go.mod)
Elasticsearch/Bleve for code indexing (github.com/blevesearch/bleve/v2 in go.mod)
Git operations via go-git (github.com/go-git/go-git/v5 in go.mod)
S3-compatible storage (AWS SDK, MinIO support in go.mod)
LDAP authentication (github.com/go-ldap/ldap/v3 in go.mod)
api_endpoints: POST /api/v1/user/keys — Add SSH key to user account
sdk_languages: Go (code.gitea.io/sdk/gitea v0.24.1 - official SDK in go.mod)
CLI tool 'tea' for command-line interaction (mentioned in README)
auth_methods: Local user accounts with bcrypt password hashing (models/auth/)
SSH key authentication for Git operations (models/asymkey/ssh_key.go)
API token authentication for REST API access (models/auth/access_token.go)
integrations: PostgreSQL database via lib/pq driver (github.com/lib/pq in go.mod)
Redis for caching and sessions (github.com/redis/go-redis/v9 in go.mod)
AWS S3 for object storage (github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2 in go.mod)
Elasticsearch for code indexing (github.com/elastic/go-elasticsearch/v8 in go.mod)
SMTP email services for notifications (github.com/jordan-wright/email in go.mod)

architecture.data_flow: HTTP requests flow through chi router middleware to handlers, which interact with XORM models for database operations. Git operations are handled through modules/git package interfacing with filesystem repositories. Background tasks are queued via modules/queue and processed asynchronously. File uploads go through storage abstraction to filesystem, S3, or other configured backends.
architecture.persistence: XORM ORM with support for SQLite, PostgreSQL, MySQL, MSSQL. Database migrations in models/migrations/. Git repositories stored on filesystem. Optional Redis for sessions/cache. Configurable object storage backends.
architecture.concurrency_model: Traditional Go goroutines with HTTP request handling. Background job processing via queue system. Database connection pooling through XORM.
architecture.communication_patterns: HTTP REST API, WebSocket for real-time features, Git protocol over HTTP/SSH, webhook callbacks for external integrations
architecture.plugin_extension_model: Modular architecture with pluggable authentication backends, storage providers, markup renderers, and queue implementations

data_model.schema_patterns: Relational database schema with foreign key relationships. Git repositories stored as filesystem directories. Binary files in object storage.
data_model.migration_strategy: Versioned database migrations in models/migrations/ with automatic upgrade on startup

algorithms.problem_type: Git repository management, version control collaboration, and CI/CD automation
core_patterns: Repository pattern for data access (models/ packages)
Middleware pattern for HTTP request processing (modules/web/middleware/)
Queue pattern for background job processing
algorithms.complexity_indicators: Large codebase with 2878 Go files, complex database relationships, Git protocol implementation, multi-tenant organization support
key_algorithms: Git diff algorithms for code comparison (modules/git/)
Merkle tree verification for Git integrity
RBAC permission resolution for access control
algorithms.evidence: modules/git/ contains Git protocol implementation, modules/indexer/ implements search algorithms, models/perm/ handles permission calculations

security_model.authentication: Multi-backend authentication: local accounts, LDAP, OAuth2, SMTP, WebAuthn. SSH/GPG key authentication for Git operations.
security_model.authorization: Role-based access control with organization/team permissions, repository-level access controls, API token scoping
security_model.data_protection: Configurable secret encryption, HTTPS/TLS support, secure session management, password hashing with bcrypt
security_model.security_headers: CORS configuration, CSP headers, rate limiting middleware (modules/web/middleware/)
security_model.vulnerability_handling: SECURITY.md file present, dependency scanning via GitHub Actions, private security reporting

deployment.container_orchestration: Docker Compose (docker-compose support evident from docker/ directory)
deployment.environment_config: Configuration via app.ini file, environment variables, command line flags. Docker environment-to-ini script for container deployment.
deployment.scaling_model: Single instance deployment with database as bottleneck. Horizontal scaling possible with shared database and storage.

code_quality_signals.test_framework: Go testing package with testify assertions
code_quality_signals.code_style: gofmt, golangci-lint, prettier for frontend (Makefile targets)

project_health.recent_activity: Active development with frontend tooling updates (rollup to rolldown migration)
project_health.community_signals: Code of conduct, security policy, issue templates, contributing guidelines, multilingual README

information_gaps: Exact API endpoint definitions would require parsing routers/api/

It implements a monolithic web application architecture with SQLite, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MSSQL database support, featuring both web UI and REST API interfaces. The system uses a traditional MVC pattern with chi router for HTTP routing, XORM for database ORM, and includes built-in CI/CD capabilities through Gitea Actions.

functional_dna.logic_archetype_evidence: The system orchestrates complex multi-step workflows across Git operations, issue tracking, pull request reviews, and CI/CD pipelines while maintaining state across all interactions. It manages persistent entity relationships and coordinates between multiple subsystems with complex state transitions.
functional_dna.data_contract_evidence: Evidence: Strict database schema with foreign key relationships (models/migrations/), strongly typed Go structs throughout models/, TypeScript interfaces in frontend, and formal REST API contracts with versioned endpoints (/api/v1/)
functional_dna.core_algorithmic_evidence: Evidence from Git DAG operations (modules/git/), dependency resolution in CI/CD pipelines, permission inheritance through organization hierarchies, and tree-walking for repository browsing and diff calculations
functional_dna.concurrency_evidence: Evidence from XORM database transactions, foreign key constraints in schema, database migration system for consistency, and support for ACID-compliant databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MSSQL)
functional_dna.abstract_problem: Orchestrates collaborative workflows around versioned document trees with branching/merging semantics, managing complex approval processes, automated task execution, and maintaining audit trails across multiple concurrent modification streams.
functional_dna.data_flow_pattern: Event-driven state transitions with persistent audit logging: User actions → validation → state mutation → notification broadcast → background task scheduling → external webhook delivery
functional_dna.repurposing_reasoning: The core patterns are highly reusable: workflow orchestration, approval processes, versioned document management, and collaborative review systems. The Git-specific components (modules/git/) are domain-locked, but the broader orchestration patterns, authentication systems, notification mechanisms, and workflow engines are generic. The modular architecture with pluggable backends makes it adaptable to different storage and authentication systems.

evidence_claims.claim: System implements sophisticated workflow orchestration with state management
evidence_claims.evidence: models/pull/ for pull request workflows, models/actions/ for CI/CD orchestration, models/issues/ for issue state transitions

evidence_claims.claim: Supports pluggable authentication backends for multi-tenant scenarios
evidence_claims.evidence: modules/auth/ with LDAP, OAuth2, SMTP, WebAuthn support, models/auth/ for token management

evidence_claims.claim: Implements comprehensive audit logging and notification system
evidence_claims.evidence: models/activities/ for activity feeds, models/webhook/ for external notifications, web_src/js/features/notification.ts

evidence_claims.claim: Uses graph algorithms for dependency and relationship management
evidence_claims.evidence: Git DAG operations in modules/git/, organization hierarchy in models/organization/, permission inheritance patterns

cross_industry_applications: Legal Document Management - collaborative review, version control, approval workflows for contracts
Academic Research Collaboration - paper drafts, peer review, version tracking, institutional access control
Manufacturing Process Control - change management workflows, approval chains, audit trails for specifications
Medical Records Systems - collaborative patient care, approval workflows, audit logging, role-based access
Financial Compliance Systems - document review workflows, approval processes, audit trails, regulatory reporting
Content Management Platforms - editorial workflows, review processes, publishing pipelines, contributor management
Architectural/Engineering Design - collaborative design review, change approval, version control for blueprints
Regulatory Affairs Management - submission workflows, review processes, compliance tracking, multi-stakeholder coordination

friction_report.migration_path: Extract core workflow orchestration patterns and authentication systems while replacing Git-specific components with domain-appropriate document/entity management. Retain notification, webhook, and approval workflow subsystems.

output.delivery: sync for API, async for webhooks

authentication.method: OAuth2, LDAP, SSH keys, WebAuthn, API tokens

comparable_systems: SharePoint - collaborative document management with approval workflows and role-based access control
Jira - issue tracking with complex workflows, state transitions, and approval processes
Confluence - collaborative content creation with review cycles and permission hierarchies
Box/Dropbox Business - file collaboration with version control and approval workflows
Adobe Creative Cloud - collaborative creative asset management with review and approval processes
information_gaps: Specific performance characteristics under concurrent load
Detailed CI/CD pipeline execution engine architecture
